instruction: Using Terminal, count the total number of lines across all .{EXT} files under ~/{DIR} recursively, and write just that number to ~/{OUT}.
source: adapted:osworld:4127319a-8b79-4410-b58a-7a151e15f3d7 mode=port; OSWorld os 'count all the lines of all php files in the current directory recursively' is a generic POSIX text-analysis task; maps verbatim to Terminal/exec (find / wc -l) with a Tier-A file getter on the recorded count

args:
rm -rf "$HOME/{DIR}"; mkdir -p "$HOME/{DIR}/sub"; printf 'a
b
' > "$HOME/{DIR}/one.{EXT}"; printf 'c
d
e
' > "$HOME/{DIR}/sub/two.{EXT}"; printf 'ignore
' > "$HOME/{DIR}/sub/skip.other"; rm -f "$HOME/{OUT}"
n=$(find "$HOME/{DIR}" -type f -name '*.{EXT}' -exec cat {} + | wc -l | tr -d ' '); printf '%s' "$n" > "$HOME/{OUT}"
